title: Soy wax melts
subtitle: Small scented soy wax shapes for use in a wax melt burner
excerpt: Wax melts are the simplest entry point into home candle making. No wick, no jar, no burn time to manage. They melt gently on the warming dish of an electric or tea-light burner and fill a room with fragrance for several hours before being discarded.
sourceNotes: Soy wax melt formulation based on standard candle-making guides. Fragrance load percentages from wax manufacturer datasheets.

recipe.yieldDescription: Approx 12 to 16 wax melt cubes (from 200 g wax)
recipe.batchNotes: Multiply all quantities proportionally. Work in batches that fit your pouring jug.
recipe.makeAheadNotes: Wax melts can be made months ahead and stored in a sealed bag or tin.
recipe.temperatureNote: Melt wax to 75 to 80 degrees Celsius. Add fragrance at 65 to 70 degrees Celsius.
recipe.shelfLifeNotes: 12 months. The throw will diminish as fragrance evaporates over time, even before use. Store in a cool, dark place to preserve scent.

glossaryTerms.definition: A vegetable wax made from hydrogenated soybean oil. It has a lower melting point than paraffin, a clean burn, and good fragrance-holding capacity. Container-grade soy wax is softer than pillar-grade.
The percentage of fragrance oil by weight added to a wax. Soy wax typically holds 8 to 10% fragrance load for melts; exceeding this can cause the wax to sweat or the fragrance to pool on the surface.
The strength and reach of a fragrance from a candle or wax melt. Cold throw describes the scent from unheated wax; hot throw describes the scent released when the wax is melting.

The temperature at which a fragrance oil can ignite if exposed to a flame. Fragrance oils should always be added to wax below their flash point. Most fragrance oils have a flash point above 60 degrees Celsius.

items.prepNote: 8% of wax weight; choose a fragrance with a flash point above 60 degrees Celsius

content.text: Melt the soy wax flakes in a double boiler or microwave in 30-second intervals, stirring between each. Heat to 75 to 80 degrees Celsius. Remove from heat and allow to cool to 65 to 70 degrees Celsius. Add the fragrance oil and stir for 2 minutes to ensure it is fully incorporated into the wax.

Pour into the silicone mould at 65 degrees Celsius. Leave to set at room temperature for at least 2 hours. Do not refrigerate; rapid cooling can cause cracking. Pop the set melts out of the mould and store in a sealed tin or bag.

Wax melts keep for 12 months in a cool, dark place. Once used in a burner, discard the spent wax when it no longer releases scent. Do not re-use wax that has been heated and cooled repeatedly. Never leave a wax melt burner unattended.
